# hermes-prime

> A bash bootstrap that injects a versioned CLAUDE.md fragment into a project so a fresh Claude Code session opens with grounding triggers, convention pointers, and tool-map already in scope. Per-project, marker-anchored, idempotent, fully reversible.

## What it does

Four subcommands on a single bash binary:

- `--check`: verify prereqs (hermes-ground, hermes-rubric-blinded, handbook, 4 feedback memory files).
- `--print`: print the CLAUDE-fragment to stdout.
- `--inject <project>`: append the fragment to `<project>/CLAUDE.md` between markers; backs up any existing CLAUDE.md.
- `--uninject <project>`: remove the marked block, or restore from latest backup.

Idempotent. Re-running `--inject` is a no-op (size delta ≤ 1 char).

## Install

    git clone https://github.com/hermes-labs-ai/hermes-prime.git
    ln -s "$PWD/hermes-prime/bin/hermes-session-init" /usr/local/bin/hermes-session-init

## What gets injected

A markdown block containing:
- 7 grounding-trigger conditions (when to call hermes-ground)
- Pointers to 4 standing-feedback memory files (calibrate-before-ship, rubric pass-through, grounding-on-emergence, no-noun-phrase-before-file)
- Tool map (hermes-ground, hermes-rubric-blinded, handbook, this bootstrap)
- The rule most often forgotten mid-session: no noun-phrase label until a file exists at a path.

See `CLAUDE-fragment.md` for the full text.

## When to use

- Starting recursive/emergent work (taxonomy design, rubric construction, structure-discovery debugging).
- Onboarding a fresh `claude --print` subagent that needs the parent session's conventions.
- Restarting a long session in a new context.

## When not to use

- Short single-task sessions where the conventions add noise without payoff.
- Generation-task-only sessions with no grounding or rubric loop.

## Status

v0.1.0 — alpha. Mechanism fully tested (9/9 unit assertions green). Empirical convention-recall claim in `evals/`.

## Related

- hermes-ground — fresh-context grounding agent (the tool the fragment teaches the orchestrator to invoke).
- hermes-rubric — evidence-first scoring; rubric pass-through pattern.
- hermes-blind — multi-turn drift correction; runs *after* drift accumulates. hermes-prime runs *before*.
- hermes-seal — cryptographic attestation; this repo ships a sealed manifest.

## Repo

https://github.com/hermes-labs-ai/hermes-prime
